summary.intent: Fix xterm terminal scroll anchoring and add auto-resume behavior when navigating to a session detail page, plus make the session ID in the breadcrumb copyable to clipboard.
summary.outcome: All three features were implemented: xterm now stays anchored to the bottom via scrollToBottom() callbacks, navigating to a session auto-runs 'claude --resume <sessionId>', and the session ID breadcrumb is clickable with a copy-to-clipboard tooltip.

repo: xterm's write() is asynchronous internally; scrollToBottom() must be called in the write callback, not after the write call, to ensure it runs after rendering.
fitAddon.fit() can shift the terminal viewport; scrollToBottom() must also be called after fit() to maintain scroll anchoring.
EmbeddedTerminal accepts a 'command' prop that is typed into the shell ~300ms after WebSocket connects.
Breadcrumb crumbs are managed via BreadcrumbContext and rendered in Shell.tsx; the Crumb interface was extended with an optional copyValue field to support copy-on-click behavior.
Session ID (full UUID) lives on the session object as s.sessionId and is available in SessionDetail.tsx.

code.finding: write() callback used for scrollToBottom(); also scrollToBottom() called after fitAddon.fit() to prevent viewport drift.
Crumb interface extended with optional copyValue?: string field.
New CopyableCrumb component added to render crumbs with copyValue as clickable elements with tooltip feedback ('Copied!').
Passes copyValue: s.sessionId to the last breadcrumb crumb and command={'claude --resume <sessionId>'} to EmbeddedTerminal.

workflow: Multiple edits to Shell.tsx were needed in sequence (render logic, then the CopyableCrumb component definition), suggesting the file has distinct sections for component definitions vs. render logic.
Grepping for component and context patterns (setCrumbs, BreadcrumbContext) was necessary to trace the full data flow before making changes.

friction: Shell.tsx required multiple separate edits — the copy rendering logic and the CopyableCrumb component definition had to be added in distinct passes, indicating the file structure wasn't immediately obvious.
SessionDetail.tsx was read four times before all necessary context was gathered, suggesting the file is large or the relevant sections were spread out.
